arrangements.name: GBA Standard Contract (Mainland, Hong Kong)
arrangements.summary: Facilitated personal-information transfer between Hong Kong and the nine Mainland GBA cities.

arrangements.name: GBA Standard Contract (Mainland, Macao)
arrangements.summary: Facilitated personal-information transfer between Macao and the nine Mainland GBA cities (CAC, Macao DSEDT/DSPDP; effective Sept 2024).

arrangements.name: PIPL cross-border transfer
arrangements.summary: Mainland China cross-border PI: CAC security assessment, standard contract, or certification (Provisions on Promoting and Regulating Cross-Border Data Flows, 2024).

arrangements.name: GDPR Chapter V transfers
arrangements.summary: EU/EEA personal-data transfers: adequacy decision or Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs).

arrangements.name: APPI cross-border transfer (Art. 28)
arrangements.summary: Japan APPI: prior consent, a PPC-designated equivalent country (EU, UK), or a recipient with equivalent safeguards / APEC CBPR.

arrangements.name: PIPA overseas transfer (Art. 28-8)
arrangements.summary: Korea PIPA: consent, a law or treaty, contract-necessity with notice, or a PIPC-recognised certification.

arrangements.name: PDPA Transfer Limitation Obligation (s.26)
arrangements.summary: Singapore PDPA: ensure the recipient provides a standard of protection comparable to the PDPA via an enforceable contract, BCR, or certification.

arrangements.name: Australian Privacy Principle 8 (cross-border disclosure)
arrangements.summary: Australia Privacy Act: the disclosing entity stays accountable (s.16C) unless the recipient is bound by a substantially similar law with enforceable mechanisms.

arrangements.name: UK international data transfer (IDTA / Addendum)
arrangements.summary: UK GDPR Chapter V: an adequacy regulation, or appropriate safeguards via the UK IDTA or the UK Addendum to the EU SCCs.

arrangements.name: Malaysia PDPA cross-border transfer (s.129)
arrangements.summary: Malaysia PDPA (amended, in force 1 Apr 2025): a destination with substantially similar law or an adequate level of protection; see the CBPDT Guidelines.
